svg.text: PalSwap: How 4 Colors Cover Every CGA Palette
Games pick one of 4 palette variants — PalSwap pre-loads ALL of them into DAC 0–15
= Our ATC routing (DAC 1, 8, 9 — conflict-free, no overlap)
Standard entries = used by games selecting Palette 0/1 High/Low
All 16 hold the right color — no matter what the game picks!
When the game calls INT 10h AH=00 to set CGA mode 4, the BIOS normally reloads the default palette.
With 1201h/31h active, the BIOS skips this — our DAC values survive the mode switch.
The game then calls AH=0Bh to select its palette, which routes ATC to our pre-loaded DAC entries.
